volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 2.1.322b - Section 2.2.365b)

sections.label: Section 2.1.322b
sections.translation: Why so

sections.label: Section 2.1.322c
sections.translation: Ah me ! I dread to tell it ; prithee , do you tell it , Byrrhia .

sections.label: Section 2.1.323b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell it .

sections.label: Section 2.1.323c
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.324
sections.translation: He ’ s in love with your betrothed .

sections.label: Section 2.1.324b
sections.original: Nae iste haud mecum sentit : Ehodum dic mihi :
sections.translation: Assuredly he ’ s not of my way of thinking . Come now , tell me ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.325
sections.original: Numquidnam amplius tibi cum illa fuit Charine ?
sections.translation: have you had any more to do with her , Charinus ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.325b
sections.translation: Oh Pamphilus , nothing .

sections.label: Section 2.1.326b
sections.translation: How much I wish you had .

sections.label: Section 2.1.326c
sections.original: Nunc te per amicitiam et per amorem obsecro
sections.translation: Now , by our friendship and by my affection , I do beseech you , in the first place , not to marry her .

sections.label: Section 2.1.327b
sections.translation: For my own part I ’ ll use my endeavors .

sections.label: Section 2.1.327c
sections.translation: But if that can not be , or if this marriage is agreeable to you —

sections.label: Section 2.1.328b
sections.translation: Agreeable to me ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.328c
sections.translation: Put it off for some days at least , while I go elsewhere , that I may not be witness .

sections.label: Section 2.1.329b
sections.translation: Now listen , once for all :

sections.label: Section 2.1.330
sections.original: Ego Charine neutiquam officium liberi esse hominis puto
sections.translation: I think it , Charinus , to be by no means the part of an ingenuous man , when he confers nothing , to expect that it should be considered as an obligation on his part . I am more desirous to avoid this match , than you to gain it .

sections.label: Section 2.1.333
sections.translation: You have restored me to life .

sections.label: Section 2.1.333b
sections.original: Nunc si quid potes aut tute aut hic Byrrhia ,
sections.translation: Now , if you can do any thing , either you yourself , or Byrrhia here , manage , fabricate , invent , contrive some means , whereby she may be given to you ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.335
sections.original: Ego id agam qui mihi ne detur .
sections.translation: this I shall aim at , how she may not be given to me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.335b
sections.translation: I am satisfied .

sections.label: Section 2.1.335c
sections.translation: Most opportunely I perceive Davus , on whose advice I have depended .

sections.label: Section 2.1.336b
sections.original: At tu hercle haud quidquam mihi ;
sections.translation: But you , i ’ faith , tell me nothing , [*] except those things which there is no need for knowing . ( Pushing him away .) Get you gone from here .

sections.label: Section 2.1.337b
sections.translation: Certainly I will , and with all my heart . ( Exit .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.338
sections.original: Di boni , boni quid porto ! sed ubi inveniam Pamphilum ,
sections.translation: Ye gracious Gods , what good news I bring ! But where shall I find Pamphilus , that I may remove the apprehension in which he now is , and fill his mind with joy —?

sections.label: Section 2.2.340
sections.translation: He ’ s rejoiced about something , I don ’ t know what .

sections.label: Section 2.2.340b
sections.original: Nihil est : nondum haec rescivit mala .
sections.translation: It ’ s of no consequence ; he hasn ’ t yet heard of these misfortunes .

sections.label: Section 2.2.341
sections.original: Quem ego nunc credo , si iam audierit sibi paratas nuptias —
sections.translation: For I do believe now , if he has already heard that a marriage is prepared for him —

sections.label: Section 2.2.342
sections.translation: Don ’ t you hear him ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.342b
sections.translation: He is seeking me distractedly all the city over . But where shall I look for him ? Or in which direction now first to betake me —

sections.label: Section 2.2.343b
sections.translation: Do you hesitate to accost him ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.344
sections.translation: I have it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.344b
sections.translation: Davus , come here ! Stop !

sections.label: Section 2.2.344c
sections.original: Quis homo est , qui me ? O Pamphile ,
sections.translation: Who ’ s the person that ’ s — ( Turning round .) O Pamphilus ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.345
sections.original: Te ipsum quaero . Euge Charine : ambo opportune . Vos volo .
sections.translation: you are the very man I ’ m looking for . Well done , Charinus ! both in the nick of time : I want you both .

sections.label: Section 2.2.346
sections.translation: Davus , I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 2.2.346b
sections.translation: Nay but , do hear this .

sections.label: Section 2.2.346c
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly ruined !

sections.label: Section 2.2.346d
sections.translation: I know what you are afraid of .

sections.label: Section 2.2.347
sections.original: Mea quidem hercle certe in dubio vita est .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , my life indeed is really in danger .

sections.label: Section 2.2.347b
sections.translation: And what you are afraid of , I know .

sections.label: Section 2.2.348
sections.translation: My marriage —

sections.label: Section 2.2.348b
sections.translation: As if I did not know it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.348c
sections.translation: This day —

sections.label: Section 2.2.348d
sections.translation: Why keep dinning me with it , when I know it all ? ( To PAMPHILUS .) This are you afraid of , lest you should marry her ; and you ( to CHARINUS ,) lest you should not marry her .

sections.label: Section 2.2.349b
sections.translation: You understand the matter .

sections.label: Section 2.2.350
sections.translation: That ’ s the very thing .

sections.label: Section 2.2.350b
sections.original: Atque istuc ipsum nihil pericli est : me vide .
sections.translation: And that very thing is in no danger ; trust me for that .

sections.label: Section 2.2.351
sections.original: Obsecro te , quam primum hoc me libera miserum metu .
sections.translation: I do entreat you , release wretched me as soon as possible from this apprehension .

sections.label: Section 2.2.351b
sections.translation: Well , then , I will release you ; Chremes is not going to give you his daughter at present .

sections.label: Section 2.2.352b
sections.translation: How do you know ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.352c
sections.translation: You shall know . Your father just now laid hold of me ; he said that a wife was to be given you to-day , and many other things as well , which just now I haven ’ t time to relate .

sections.label: Section 2.2.355
sections.original: Continuo ad te properans percurro ad forum ut dicam tibi haec .
sections.translation: Hastening to you immediately , I ran on to the Forum that I might tell you these things . When I didn ’ t find you , I ascended there to ahigh place . [*] I looked around ; you were nowhere . There by chance I saw Byrrhia , his servant ( pointing to CHARINUS .) . I inquired of him ; he said he hadn ’ t seen you . This puzzled me . I considered what I was to do . As I was returning in the mean time , a surmise from the circumstances themselves occurred to me : How now ,—

sections.label: Section 2.2.360
sections.original: Paululum obsoni : ipsus tristis : de improviso nuptiae :
sections.translation: a very small amount of good cheer ; he out of spirits ; a marriage all of a sudden ; these things don ’ t agree .

sections.label: Section 2.2.361b
sections.translation: But to what purpose this ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.361c
sections.translation: I forthwith betook myself to the house of Chremes . When I arrived there — stillness before the door ; [*] then I was pleased at that .

sections.label: Section 2.2.363
sections.translation: You say well .

sections.label: Section 2.2.363b
sections.translation: Proceed .

sections.label: Section 2.2.363c
sections.translation: I stopped there . In the mean time I saw no one going in , no one going out ; no matron at the house , [*]

sections.label: Section 2.2.365
sections.original: Nil ornati , nil tumulti : accessi : intro aspexi .
sections.translation: no preparation , no bustle . I drew near ; looked in —

sections.label: Section 2.2.365b
sections.translation: I understand ; a considerable indication .
